title: Rich Springy Chinese Zucchini Mu Shu
description: A rich and springy Chinese dish featuring zucchini, perfect for a date night, made with plant-based friendly ingredients and cooked on a yakimono grill in the style of mu-shu.

ingredients.name: tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the tofu and cook until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes, then set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the skillet and stir in the sliced mushrooms, cooking until they release their liquid and start to brown.
Add the sliced zucchini to the skillet and cook until it starts to soften, about 2-3 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, hoisin sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil, then pour the sauce over the zucchini and mushrooms, stirring to combine.
Add the cooked tofu back into the skillet and stir to combine with the zucchini and mushroom mixture.
Warm the mu-shu wrappers by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the mu-shu by spooning the zucchini and tofu mixture onto a wrapper, topping with chopped scallions, and serving immediately.
